case_number: constrained to observe that no case

chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH CIRCUIT COURT, LARKANA Present: Irshad Ali Shah, J. Agha Faisal, J. CP D89of 2019: Asma Waqar vs. Province of Sindh, through Chief Secretary & others For the Petitioner: Mr. Safdar Ali Ghouri, Advocate For the Respondents: Mr.Abdul Hamid Bhurgri, Addl. A.G. Dates of hearing: 22.02.2022 Date of announcement: 22.02.2022 ORDER Agha Faisal, J. The petitioner admits to have failed in the competitive process of the Sindh Public Service Commission (SPSC) and seeks to have the said result voided. Admittedly,an aggregate of 50 marks was required to qualify and per the petitioner’s quantification she obtained 50 marks; whereas per the respondents she obtained 49.5 marks.Counsel seeks to revisit the tabulation process and requires this Court to conduct the said exercise. Such a plea is untenable as it is settled law that adjudication of disputed questions of fact, requiring evidence etc., is not amenable in the exercise of writ jurisdiction. In view hereof, this Court is constrained to observe that no case has been set forth for the invocation of the discretionary writ jurisdiction of this Court, hence, this petition is hereby dismissed. JUDGE JUDGE 2016 CLC 1; 2015 PLC 45; 2015 CLD 257; 2011 SCMR 1990; 2001 SCMR 574; PLD 2001 Supreme Court 415; Per Ijaz Ul Ahsan J. in Syed Iqbal Hussain Shah Gillani vs. PBC & Others reported as 2021 SCMR 425; Muhammad Fiaz Khan vs. Ajmer Khan & Another reported as 2010 SCMR 105.
